title: artemisia norvegica var. comata
intro: Artemisia norvegica var. comata, also known as Norwegian wormwood, is a perennial plant in the daisy family that is native to Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiparasitic effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including the ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and even act as a natural insect repellent. In herbal preparations, Norwegian wormwood is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although it should be used with caution due to its potential toxicity.
